title: A Season of Her Own Making
description: New York, 1898. Cornelia Vane has been raised to be sold well — a Gilded Age daughter, a fortune in a ballgown. But Cornelia has quietly been learning the family's railroad accounts, and she knows the marriage being arranged for her would save a man who deliberately ruined her father. She decides to ruin him first. A Gilded Age romance of ledgers, ballrooms, and a woman who refuses to be a transaction.

chapters.title: An Introduction at the Astor Ball
